title: Red-Green Feature-Driven Testing SOP — encoding into the /stories skill
topic: How to harden the red-green-and-feature-driven test discipline (proven in PLA-0053/0054/0055) into the <stories> skill so it is enforced automatically and ad-hoc protocol blocks can be deleted from plan descriptions
summary: Specifies a feature-grouping layer + a new feature_test work-item kind + a tracker_group plan field that together make red-green ordering and Tracker regression-library registration first-class concerns of the /stories skill. Lists 12 concrete, ordered edits the user can apply to SKILL.md as a follow-up task.
content:
Contents

  1. A. Analysis of the current /stories skill
  2. B. Red-Green Feature-Driven SOP
  3. C. Regression-target loop
  4. D. Skill changes per gate
  5. E. Open questions and risks
  6. F. Recommended next actions

A. Analysis of the current /stories skill

The 7-gate acceptance system (.claude/skills/stories/SKILL.md:24-34) makes eight mandatory attributes per work item: story ID + title, AIGEN tag, PH-NNNN, FE-AAA-NNNN, EST-F#, RISK-LOW|MED|HIGH, PLA-NNNN, and a description with 3+ "As Proven by" criteria. An optional MULTI-AGENT tag rides alongside for parallel-safe stories.

Tests appear in exactly one place: the Step 3 confidence checklist (SKILL.md:170) requires each AC to start with an observable verb. There is no first-class concept of a test story, no required test grouping, no Tracker registration, no red-green ordering rule, no green-commit gate on implementation completion. "Done" today means the work item's status flips to completed (SKILL.md:18); nothing in the skill requires the green commit to land before that flip is legitimate.

The PlanWorkItem type (app/api/dev/plans/route.ts:5-12) carries order, title, story_id, card_url, status, and an optional notes field — no kind, no feature_id, no covers, no tracker_group. The tags array shown in every recent plan (PLA-0053.json:49-56) is persisted but is missing from the type declaration entirely — an existing schema gap.

What PLA-0053/0054/0055 added by hand and what the skill should absorb:

  • A RED-GREEN PROTOCOL block in implementation_plan[0] on every plan — same wording, copy-pasted.
  • A TRACKER REGRESSION LIBRARY block in implementation_plan[1] — same wording, copy-pasted, declaring a tracker_group name (e.g. backend-workspace-foundation).
  • A **Feature membership:** paragraph injected into every implementation story's description.
  • A trailing block of TEST(FN) stories with a hand-written 3-AC red/green/regression-lock shape and an explicit Covers stories: roster.

None of this is enforced — a future plan author who forgets one of the blocks fails the SOP silently. The skill is the right home.

B. Red-Green Feature-Driven SOP

Feature grouping rule. A feature is the smallest vertical slice that can be regression-tested end-to-end and observed as one thing being done (e.g. PLA-0053's F1 = workspace clamp end-to-end: JWT claim + resolver + schema + two services). After Step 1 parses stories the skill proposes a grouping with one feature per slice; the user can revise. Every implementation story belongs to exactly one feature; the feature has exactly one test suite.

New mandatory work-item kind: feature_test. Sits in the same work_item_backlog array as implementation stories but carries extra schema fields:

{
  "order": <ordinal>,
  "title": "NNNNN \u2014 TEST(F<N>): <suite name>",
  "story_id": "NNNNN",
  "card_url": null,
  "status": "todo",
  "kind": "feature_test",
  "feature_id": "F<N>",
  "feature_name": "<name>",
  "covers": ["<story_id>", "..."],
  "tracker_group": "<scope>-<slug>",
  "description": "<canonical 3-AC template>",
  "tags": ["AIGEN", "PH-NNNN", "FE-AAA-NNNN", "EST-F#", "RISK-LOW|MED", "PLA-NNNN"]
}

New mandatory tag on every implementation story: FEAT-N. Implementation stories without an approved FEAT-N are rejected at Step 3. The tag is allocated in Step 0 from the grouping approved at Step 1.b.

Red-green ordering rule. Inside each feature group's segment of the work_item_backlog, the feature_test work item carries the lowest order value — it is the first story in that group. Its red commit ships before any of its covers implementation stories. Each covered implementation story's status may not flip to completed until the feature_test suite is green for that story's assertions.

Feature test AC shape (canonical 3 AC, mandatory).

#CriterionProven by
1RED commit registeredtest(<area>): red \u2014 F<N> <name> [<test-story-id>] committed on base; suite fails; Tracker dashboard <tracker_group>/<feature_id> = status=fail.
2GREEN commit closes the cycleAll covers implementation stories land; the final green commit turns the suite green; Tracker run = status=pass.
3Regression library lockSuite registered in Tracker's permanent library so every future plan's pre-merge run re-executes it.

C. Regression-target loop

Tracker group naming convention. <scope>-<plan-slug> where <scope> ∈ {backend, frontend, fullstack, db} and <plan-slug> is a 2–4 word kebab slug of the plan title. Each plan declares exactly one tracker_group; every feature_test in the plan registers under it. Live examples from the motivating plans: backend-workspace-foundation (PLA-0053), frontend-chip-foundation (PLA-0054), frontend-priority-customisation (PLA-0055).

rg-runner invocation shape (pending verification). The runner lives in the sibling MMFFDev - Tracker repo and is not readable from here. Auth is via RG_API_KEY=trk_xxx (per .claude/memory/project_tracker_rg_api_key.md); the key is project-clamped to Vector so no --project flag is needed. Proposed flag shape (NOT verified):

rg-runner \
  --group <tracker_group> \
  --feature <feature_id> \
  --story <story_id> \
  --status {red|green} \
  --suite <suite-name> \
  --commit <sha>

Before PLA-0053 story 1 ships, the implementer should run cmd/rg-runner --help in the Tracker repo and update SKILL.md with the verified syntax. If --help is undocumented, file TD-RG-RUNNER-DOCS (S2, trigger: first feature_test commit).

Regression-rerun cadence. The intended model: every future plan's pre-merge gate calls rg-runner --run-all --project vector, re-executing every Tracker-registered suite. A previously-green suite going red on a future plan's run flags a regression on that plan, not on the suite's home plan. The wiring (pre-push hook or pre-merge CI step) may not exist yet — flag TD-RG-RERUN-WIRING (S2) and confirm or land it during PLA-0053.

D. Skill changes per gate

SKILL.md locationExisting behaviourReplacement / addition
SKILL.md:24-34 Hard Rules tag list 8 mandatory attributes per work item. Add #9 FEAT-N tag — feature group membership; applies to implementation stories. Add a parallel rule: every plan declares a top-level tracker_group field.
SKILL.md:106-117 Step 0 Allocate STORY_IDS, PH_TAG, FE_TAG, confirm PLAN_ID. Insert sub-step 7: Allocate feature_ids F1..Fk from the grouping proposed in Step 1.b; assign each implementation story its FEAT-N tag.
SKILL.md:121-145 Step 1 Parse stories, present numbered list for approval. Add sub-step 1.b Propose feature grouping BEFORE the approval ask. Annotate each line of the numbered list with [F<N>]. Render a grouping block under the list and ask the user to approve or revise.
SKILL.md:158-183 Step 3 confidence gate 10-line checklist with 85% threshold. Append: [ ] Feature membership (85%+): story carries a valid FEAT-N tag pointing to an approved feature group. NOT "no group", NOT "TBD". Implementation stories without FEAT-N fail the gate.
SKILL.md:228-271 Step 5 compose work items Single work_item_backlog shape + 6 mandatory tags. Add the alternative feature_test shape (above). Add FEAT-N to the implementation-story mandatory tag list. Include the canonical 3-AC red/green/regression-lock description template inline.
SKILL.md:284-355 Step 6.5 write plan JSON PlanDoc construction + write/merge + index update + self-check. Add required PlanDoc top-level field tracker_group (kebab <scope>-<slug>). Extend 6.5.d self-check with four new checks: every impl story has FEAT-N; every feature has ≥1 feature_test; every feature_test has the 3 mandatory AC; plan declares tracker_group.
NEW between 6.5 and 7 (none today) Add Step 6.6 \u2014 Feature-test parity check: assert exactly one feature_test per approved feature; assert every story_id in covers appears in this run OR is already completed in the merged target.
SKILL.md:359-377 Step 7 report Per-story line with tag list. Add a feature-coverage block:
Tracker group: <name>
Feature groups: K | feature tests: K | impl stories: N
F1 \u2014 <name> (test NNNNN covers id,id)
Add a \u26a0 feature group F<N> has no feature_test warning condition.

E. Open questions and risks

  1. Exact rg-runner flag syntax is unknown. Sibling repo, not readable here. The skill should encode the contract (red POST first, green POST closes, library lock requires both); the implementer verifies cmd/rg-runner --help at PLA-0053 story 1 and updates SKILL.md with the verified command. Filed as TD-RG-RUNNER-DOCS.
  2. Interaction with feedback_stories_all_layers. That memory mandates decomposition across backend/frontend/migration/tests before invoking <stories>. No conflict: stories_all_layers is the spread rule (all layers represented); the new SOP is the clustering rule (stories grouped into features). They compose. A new memory entry should link the two so future readers see both halves.
  3. Stories that don't naturally belong to a feature. Recommended approach (Option B): every story must belong to a feature, but a feature can be 1-story. For a single doc page or a standalone migration, the feature_test is calibrated to what's observable (a make migrate smoke, a npm run build doc-render check, etc.). Rejected alternatives: (a) EXEMPT-FEAT flag — creates a loophole; (b) blanket exemption by feature-area tag — too coarse.
  4. Failure mode: red commit lands, implementation never does. Three responses, all required:
    (a) the skill must reject feature_test stories that have no covers implementation in the same /stories invocation — feature tests cannot be authored in isolation;
    (b) every long-lived red commit gets a TD-RED-<story_id> entry (S2) with trigger "close when feature ships OR delete suite on date X if descoped";
    (c) Tracker should support a descoped state distinct from fail so deliberately-orphaned suites stop polluting regression reruns. Tracker-side change, filed as TD-RG-DESCOPED-STATE.
  5. Backward compatibility with PLA-0053/0054/0055. Recommended approach (Option B): grandfather them. The three plans encode the SOP in description prose and Feature membership: markers; they don't carry the new schema fields. Ship a one-shot dev/scripts/migrate-plans-to-feature-sop.sh that parses the existing markers, derives tracker_group from the existing implementation_plan[1] text, and rewrites the plan JSON in place. Idempotent. No new IDs allocated; the existing PLA-NNNNs persist.
  6. Tag tax. Implementation stories already carry 6 mandatory tags; FEAT-N makes 7. Mitigation: render FEAT-N in a dedicated column in the Plans tab rather than mixing it into the tag bag, and consider promoting feature_id to a first-class PlanWorkItem field (alongside kind) rather than relying on the tags array.
  7. Test estimation discipline. Feature_test stories estimate by suite complexity (typically F3–F5). They are NOT folded into the parent implementation stories' EST. A feature_test that estimates F8+ is a signal the feature is too big and the implementation stories should be re-split.
  8. PlanDoc schema gap. PlanWorkItem in app/api/dev/plans/route.ts:5-12 is missing the tags field that every plan persists. The SOP work should also tighten that type (add tags: string[], kind?: "implementation" | "feature_test", feature_id?: string, feature_name?: string, covers?: string[]) and extend PlanDoc with tracker_group?: string. Filed as TD-PLANDOC-FEATURE-SOP.
  9. Step \u22121 overlap scan. Already scans dev/plans/ regardless of schema, so it keeps working unchanged. But tracker_group becomes a new dedup vector: two active plans should not declare the same tracker_group unless deliberately co-shipping. Step \u22121.c should warn if a candidate match collides on tracker_group too.

F. Recommended next actions

Twelve concrete, in-order edits. Each touches one specific section of one specific file.

  1. Edit .claude/skills/stories/SKILL.md:24-34 — add tag #9 FEAT-N and a parallel rule "plan declares top-level tracker_group". Update the "a work-item missing any of (1)-(8) is a defect" line to read (1)-(9).
  2. Edit .claude/skills/stories/SKILL.md:121-145 (Step 1) — insert sub-step 1.b Propose feature grouping before the approval ask. Render a grouping block under the numbered list; annotate each line with [F<N>]; ask the user to approve or revise.
  3. Edit .claude/skills/stories/SKILL.md:106-117 (Step 0) — insert sub-step 7 "Allocate feature_ids F1..Fk; assign FEAT-N tags" and add FEAT_TAGS to the self-check.
  4. Edit .claude/skills/stories/SKILL.md:158-183 (Step 3) — append the [ ] Feature membership (85%+) checklist line. Implementation stories without FEAT-N fail the gate.
  5. Edit .claude/skills/stories/SKILL.md:228-271 (Step 5) — add the feature_test work-item shape with kind, feature_id, feature_name, covers, tracker_group. Add FEAT-N to the implementation-story tag list. Inline the canonical 3-AC red/green/regression-lock description template.
  6. Edit .claude/skills/stories/SKILL.md:284-355 (Step 6.5.a) — add required PlanDoc field tracker_group (kebab <scope>-<slug>).
  7. Edit .claude/skills/stories/SKILL.md:348-355 (Step 6.5.d) — add four new self-check items: every impl story has FEAT-N; every feature has ≥1 feature_test; every feature_test has 3 mandatory AC; plan declares tracker_group following the convention.
  8. Insert new section between Steps 6.5 and 7 in SKILL.md — Step 6.6 Feature-test parity check: one feature_test per approved feature; every covers entry exists in this run or is already completed in the merged target; reject the run on failure.
  9. Edit .claude/skills/stories/SKILL.md:359-377 (Step 7 report) — add the feature-coverage block: Tracker group, Feature groups: K | feature tests: K | impl stories: N, one line per feature with covered story IDs, and a \u26a0 warning when a group has no feature_test.
  10. Create dev/scripts/migrate-plans-to-feature-sop.sh — one-shot backfill for PLA-0053/0054/0055. Parses Feature membership: F<N> markers from each story's description, derives tracker_group from implementation_plan[1], rewrites the JSON with the new schema fields. Idempotent. Does not allocate new IDs.
  11. Create .claude/memory/feedback_feature_driven_testing_sop.md — encode the SOP itself as a feedback memory entry. Body: "Every story belongs to a feature group; every feature has exactly one feature_test suite; red-green ordering enforced by /stories." Why: Rick 2026-05-16; tests live at the feature level, not the story level; Tracker is the long-term regression library. Link [[feedback_red_green_always]], [[feedback_stories_all_layers]], [[feedback_stories_system]]. Add an entry to MEMORY.md.
  12. File three TD entries in docs/c_tech_debt.md — TD-RG-RUNNER-DOCS (S2, trigger: PLA-0053 story 1 ships): verify cmd/rg-runner --help in the Tracker repo and patch SKILL.md with the verified command. TD-RG-RERUN-WIRING (S2): confirm or land the pre-push / pre-merge hook that runs rg-runner --run-all --project vector. TD-PLANDOC-FEATURE-SOP (S2): extend the PlanWorkItem and PlanDoc types in app/api/dev/plans/route.ts to match the new schema (tags, kind, feature_id, feature_name, covers, tracker_group).

Sequencing: 1\u20139 are pure SKILL.md edits and can ship as one PR. 10 is a one-shot script run before the first new SOP-conformant plan is authored. 11 is a memory write — can land alongside the SKILL.md edits. 12 is three TD entries filed at the same time as the SKILL.md edits, with the verification action falling on PLA-0053 story 1.
